mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month rise in FEDFUNDS reflects an active monetary tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 60-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following FEDFUNDS increases are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more over a trailing three-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes broader growth slowdowns, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as risk premia rise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >= 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average post-trigger return is not statistically distinguishable from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates simultaneous weakening of consumer spending and residential investment, two dominant demand channels, which jointly signal an impending broad-based demand slowdown that markets underprice, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. Neither series alone carries the same signal strength as their joint decline.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce negative mean 20-day forward returns significantly different from periods where only one series declines (p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the interaction term stays below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle monetary tightening regime (elevated and rising FEDFUNDS), accelerating INDPRO growth is interpreted by markets as evidence of an overheating economy that will provoke further Fed tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns. In an easing or early-cycle regime, the same INDPRO acceleration signals healthy expansion and produces positive returns instead.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising for >= 6 consecutive months and above its 12-month moving average
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with overheating risk
failure_condition: The direction of the INDPRO-return relationship does not flip sign between tightening and easing regimes when tested via regime-split regression, or the tightening-regime coefficient is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects continued labor market expansion and household income growth, supporting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or periods of positive PAYEMS growth do not show significantly higher mean forward returns than periods of contraction (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 4%, markets price in an elevated probability of near-term Fed tightening surprises, increasing short-horizon volatility and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as rate-hike risk premia are rapidly repriced.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following CPI YoY > 4% readings are not significantly more negative than unconditional 5-day returns (p > 0.05), or triggered instances show positive returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 quarterly growth is positive and coincides with rising RSAFS, the combination confirms broad-based consumption-led real GDP expansion that reinforces corporate revenue growth expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. GDPC1 alone is too lagged and low-frequency to carry this signal without confirming RSAFS momentum.
failure_condition: The joint GDPC1-RSAFS positive-growth signal fails to produce mean 60-day forward returns significantly above the unconditional mean (p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the interaction term stays below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflation-like regime where UNRATE remains low (tight labor market) while INDPRO declines for two or more consecutive months, markets interpret this as a supply-side squeeze that raises input costs without offsetting demand destruction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns as margin compression fears rise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for >= 2 consecutive months while UNRATE stays below 4.5%
regime_hint: stagflation regime with tight labor market and weakening industrial output
failure_condition: Triggered instances of simultaneous INDPRO decline and low UNRATE do not produce significantly negative mean 60-day forward returns compared to baseline (p > 0.05), or the effect disappears when tested outside periods with rising CPIAUCSL.
